LAW AND MOTION 302,DEFENDANT KRISZTINA EMODI'S MOTION TO SEAL, REMOVE, OR DELETE PREVIOUSLY FILED INITIAL COMPLAINT IS DENIED. MS. EMODI FAILS TO SHOW AN OVERRIDING INTEREST THAT TRUMPS THE PRESUMPTIVE RIGHT OF PUBLIC ACCESS TO ALL COURT DOCUMENTS, ESPECIALLY SINCE THE DOCUMENT SHE SEEKS TO MAKE UNAVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC HAS BEEN PUBLICLY AVAILABLE FOR APPROXIMATELY A YEAR AND A HALF. IF MS. EMODI'S SHOWING WAS SUFFICIENT, THEN ALL PREVAILING DEFENDANTS WHO ASSERT AN ACTUAL OR POTENTIAL COLLATERAL CONSEQUENCE FROM HAVING BEEN SUED WOULD BE ENTITLED TO HAVE THE COMPLAINTS AGAINST THEM SEALED. THIS IS FAR BEYOND WHAT THE SEALING RULES CONTEMPLATE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600 =302/HK

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-17-2018 Continued To Aug-21-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion To Seal, Remove, Or Delete Previously Filed Inital Complaint
Off Calendar May-16-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Robert V. Chin, Tel. No. 1-415-256-8188 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Sinni B. Thai of the Bennett, Samuesen & Gelini, Tel. No. 1-510-444-7688
appearing for the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Krisztina Emodi's motion to seal, remove, or delete previously filed initial complaint is denied. Ms. Emodi fails to show an overriding interest that trumps the presumptive right of public access to all court documents, especially since the document she seeks to make unavailable to the public has been publicly available for approximately a year and a half. If Ms. Emodi's showing was sufficient, then all prevailing defendants who assert an actual or potential collateral consequence from having been sued would be entitled to have the complaints against them sealed. This is far beyond what the sealing rules contemplate. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-19-2018 Continued To May-22-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-16-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert Chin, Esq. 415-256-8188 for Plaintiff ANDRE ASTABIE.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ANDRE ASTABIE's MOTION FOR ORDER TO GRANT LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT (CCP SECTION 473)

Plaintiff Astabie's motion to amend complaint is GRANTED. No opposition filed.

Order is signed.(see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR#7489, mgheno@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/RBU)
